I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server mode applied globally
(all pages are interactive by default).

I need to add ASP.NET Core Identity-based authentication. Specifically:

1. A /account/login page where users can sign in with email and password
   using SignInManager. For now, just build the page with a login form
   that calls SignInManager.PasswordSignInAsync — I'll wire up the
   database and user seeding later. I just need the page structure and
   the sign-in call to be correct.

2. A /account/register page where users can create an account using
   UserManager.CreateAsync. Same deal — build the page with a
   registration form that calls the right APIs.

3. The rest of the app should remain fully interactive. Only the
   authentication pages should be set up in whatever way they need to
   work correctly with SignInManager and UserManager.

4. Make sure App.razor and routing are configured so that navigating
   to these account pages actually works — don't just add the pages
   and hope for the best.

Set up the Identity services in Program.cs. You can use an in-memory
database for the EF store if needed.

arguments.description: Add Identity and EF Core InMemory packages

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Add Identity and EF Core InMemory packages

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the setup. This is a .NET 11 Blazor Web App with global Interactive Server rendering. The key challenge with Identity is that `SignInManager` and `UserManager` rely on HTTP context (cookies), which isn't available during interactive (SignalR) rendering. So the account pages need to render statically (SSR).

Here's my plan:
1. Add NuGet packages for Identity and EF Core InMemory
2. Update Program.cs with Identity services, EF, auth middleware
3. Update App.razor to conditionally render account pages as static SSR
4. Create Login and Register pages with `@rendermode` set to null (static SSR)


5. For redirects after authentication, I'll use `NavigationManager` to handle the flow

The key pattern in .NET 8+ is that Identity pages must be SSR since `SignInManager` sets cookies through `HttpContext`. I need to mark these pages to opt out of interactive routing, either with an attribute or by explicitly setting the render mode, and then have App.razor check the endpoint metadata to determine whether to use interactive or static rendering.

Now I'm setting up the implementation: adding the required NuGet packages for Identity and Entity Framework, creating the database context, configuring Identity services and middleware in Program.cs, building the Login and Register pages as static SSR components, and updating App.razor to properly route between interactive and static pages based on the page's render mode configuration. The `ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting` attribute was introduced in .NET 9, so I can use that to force server-side rendering for the Identity pages. For form handling, I'll decorate the pages with this attribute, use `[SupplyParameterFromForm]` for binding form data, call the appropriate `SignInManager` or `UserManager` methods, and handle redirects with `NavigationManager.NavigateTo` using `forceLoad: true` to ensure a full page reload after authentication.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <BasePath />
    <ResourcePreloader />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="@Assets["lib/bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css"]" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="@Assets["app.css"]" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="@Assets["sv-989eb9354de743a383a8a39697841a96.styles.css"]" />
    <ImportMap />
    <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png" />
    <HeadOutlet @rendermode="@PageRenderMode" />
</head>

<body>
    <Routes @rendermode="@PageRenderMode" />
    @if (PageRenderMode is not null)
    {
        <ReconnectModal />
    }
    <script src="@Assets["_framework/blazor.web.js"]"></script>
</body>

</html>

@code {
    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    private IComponentRenderMode? PageRenderMode =>
        HttpContext.GetEndpoint()?.Metadata.GetMetadata<ExcludeFromInteractiveRoutingAttribute>() is not null
            ? null
            : InteractiveServer;
}

data.content:
Build succeeds. The BL0008 warnings are about `[SupplyParameterFromForm]` properties having initializers — this is standard for SSR form binding and is benign (the model binder creates the instance on POST).

Here's what was set up:

**New files:**
- `Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs` — EF Core context extending `IdentityDbContext`
- `Components/Pages/Account/Login.razor` — SSR login page at `/account/login`
- `Components/Pages/Account/Register.razor` — SSR register page at `/account/register`

**Modified files:**
- `Program.cs` — Added Identity services, EF InMemory provider, auth/authz middleware, and DB seeding
- `Components/App.razor` — Uses `[ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]` metadata to dynamically switch: account pages render as **static SSR** (required for `SignInManager`/`UserManager` which need `HttpContext`), all other pages stay **Interactive Server**

The key architectural point: `SignInManager` sets authentication cookies via `HttpContext`, which isn't available over SignalR. The `[ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]` attribute on the account pages forces them to render as static SSR, while `App.razor` detects this and conditionally applies `InteractiveServer` only to non-excluded pages.
